---
title: About
about:
  id: hero-heading
  image: img/profile_10132025.jpg
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/Liam-W-Smith
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/liam-smith-b159791b3/
    - icon: img/orcid.svg
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0003-2811-1044
---

Hey there!
My name is Liam and I’m an Assistant Data Scientist at the [Inventory & Monitoring Division](https://www.nps.gov/im/index.htm) of the National Park Service.
I support ecological research on public lands by maintaining long-term environmental datasets, assisting data managers in Alaska with all stages of the data lifecycle, from collection to publication.
Recent projects include configuring a QField application for bald eagle surveys, writing quality control and ETL scripts for water chemistry data, and developing an R-Shiny dashboard to visualize patterns in park visitation. 

I grew up in Rochester, New York alongside my four siblings and studied geography and mathematics at Middlebury College. 
When I’m not at work you can find me jumping on a trampoline, standing on my hands, or playing the piano.

To get in touch, please drop me a line at [1liam1smith1@gmail.com](mailto: 1liam1smith1@gmail.com).
